pi_items.source: Confirmed in docker-compose.yml line 10 volume mount '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web:/config:Z' and standard Docker Jitsi Meet setup pattern

Confirmed as official Google STUN server from multiple sources including Jitsi Meet documentation examples showing Google STUN servers in stunServers configuration; port 19302 is the official port for Google STUN servers

Confirmed as official Google STUN server from multiple sources including DEV Community Google STUN server list, VideoSDK documentation, and Jitsi Meet configuration examples showing stun1.l.google.com:19302 as a valid secondary Google STUN server
Exact path found in export_result.sh line 35; standard user home directory Documents path

privileged_info_summary: The task modifies a real Jitsi Meet configuration file at the standard Docker location /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js to add two official Google STUN servers (stun.l.google.com:19302 and stun1.l.google.com:19302) to the P2P stunServers configuration block. Google STUN servers stun.l.google.com and stun1.l.google.com are publicly-available official servers operated by Google and used widely in WebRTC applications including Jitsi Meet; they operate on the standard port 19302 for STUN. The task requires verification through a screenshot saved to /home/ga/Documents/stun_config_verified.png. The data is real (based on actual Jitsi Meet software and real Google public infrastructure).
